name: Digital Literacy
description: An essential course developing the skills to navigate, evaluate, create, and communicate in digital environments, covering internet safety, cybersecurity, productivity tools, AI literacy, and digital citizenship.

modules.title: Introduction to Digital Literacy
modules.objectives: Define digital literacy and its importance in modern life; identify the key components of digital literacy; understand how digital skills impact education, employment, and citizenship.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — What Is Digital Literacy?
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Digital Literacy in the 21st Century
modules.assignment: Write a one-page personal digital audit: list the digital tools you use weekly, categorize them (communication, learning, entertainment, productivity), and reflect on which skills you would most like to improve.

modules.title: Navigating the Internet Safely
modules.objectives: Identify risks of online activity including scams, phishing, and malicious websites; use safe browsing practices; understand HTTPS, URLs, and how to verify website legitimacy.
modules.video2Note: YouTube — How to Stay Safe Online
modules.assignment: Examine three websites (one legitimate, one suspicious, one clearly fake); use a checklist of trust indicators (HTTPS, domain age, contact info, grammar, design) to evaluate each and write a brief analysis of how you identified each.

modules.title: Evaluating Online Information
modules.objectives: Apply the SIFT method to evaluate digital information; identify misinformation, disinformation, and clickbait; fact-check claims using lateral reading and trusted fact-checking tools.
modules.video2Note: YouTube — How to Spot Fake News
modules.assignment: Find three viral social media posts making factual claims; use lateral reading and fact-checking sites (Snopes, FactCheck.org) to verify or debunk each claim; write a report on your findings and what made each post believable or not.

modules.title: Online Privacy & Data
modules.objectives: Understand what personal data is collected online and how it is used; adjust privacy settings on common platforms; understand cookies, tracking, and data brokers; know your digital rights.
modules.assignment: Conduct a personal privacy audit: review and adjust the privacy settings on three apps or social media platforms you use; write a summary of what data each was collecting and what changes you made.

modules.title: Cybersecurity Fundamentals
modules.objectives: Describe common cyber threats including phishing, malware, ransomware, and social engineering; create strong passwords and use multi-factor authentication; understand how to protect personal and organizational data.
modules.assignment: Evaluate the security of five accounts you use regularly (school email, social media, banking app, game account, shopping site); grade each on password strength, MFA, and recovery options; create a personal cybersecurity improvement plan.

modules.title: Productivity Software: Docs, Sheets, Slides
modules.titleZh: 生产力软件：文档、表格、演示
modules.objectives: Create, format, and collaborate on documents using Google Docs or Microsoft Word; build basic spreadsheets with formulas; design clear presentation slides; use comments, sharing, and version history.
modules.readingNote: GCFGlobal — Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides Tutorials
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Google Sheets and Docs Introduction
modules.video2Note: YouTube — Google Workspace for Students Tutorial
modules.assignment: Create a research summary document in Google Docs with proper headings and citations; build a simple budget spreadsheet in Google Sheets using SUM, AVERAGE, and IF formulas; design a three-slide presentation in Google Slides summarizing a topic of your choice.

modules.title: Digital Communication & Netiquette
modules.objectives: Write effective professional emails; understand netiquette rules for online communication; navigate online collaboration tools (Slack, Teams, Google Meet); recognize and avoid cyberbullying.
modules.assignment: Write three emails: one to a teacher requesting an extension, one to a potential employer asking about an internship, and one to a classmate coordinating a group project; apply professional email conventions to each.

modules.title: Digital Research Skills
modules.objectives: Use advanced Google search operators; find scholarly sources using Google Scholar and library databases; evaluate source credibility using SIFT; organize digital research with citation management tools.
modules.readingNote: GCFGlobal — How to Do Research Online
modules.video2Note: YouTube — How to Use Google Search Like a Pro
modules.assignment: Research a health topic using at least four different search strategies (general Google, Google Scholar, library database, Wikipedia for leads only); compare the quality and depth of results from each approach.

quizQuestions.question: Digital literacy is best described as:
options: The ability to type quickly
The ability to safely and effectively use digital technologies to find, evaluate, create, and communicate information
Knowing how to write computer code
Having access to a smartphone
quizQuestions.answer: The ability to safely and effectively use digital technologies to find, evaluate, create, and communicate information
quizQuestions.explanation: Digital literacy encompasses the full range of skills needed to function competently in a digital world — from finding and evaluating information to creating content and communicating responsibly.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is an example of a digital literacy skill?
options: Typing speed
Identifying a phishing email
Owning a computer
Having a social media account
quizQuestions.answer: Identifying a phishing email
quizQuestions.explanation: Identifying phishing emails requires critical evaluation of digital communication — a core digital literacy competency involving security awareness and source evaluation.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Digital literacy is best described as:" is: The ability to safely and effectively use digital technologies to find, evaluate, create, and communicate information.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The ability to safely and effectively use digital technologies to find, evaluate, create, and communicate information is correct.

quizQuestions.question: HTTPS in a website address indicates:
options: The website is run by the government
The connection between your browser and the website is encrypted
The website was created recently
The website has no advertisements
quizQuestions.answer: The connection between your browser and the website is encrypted
quizQuestions.explanation: HTTPS (HyperText Transfer Protocol Secure) means data transmitted between your browser and the server is encrypted — protecting information like passwords and credit card numbers.

quizQuestions.question: Phishing is a cybercrime that involves:
options: Downloading pirated software
Tricking people into revealing personal information through fake emails or websites
Using someone's Wi-Fi without permission
Sending spam messages to random addresses
quizQuestions.answer: Tricking people into revealing personal information through fake emails or websites
quizQuestions.explanation: Phishing uses deceptive emails, texts, or websites that impersonate legitimate organizations to steal login credentials, financial information, or personal data.

quizQuestions.question: A trustworthy website is MOST likely to have:
options: Many pop-up advertisements
A padlock icon, clear contact information, and professional content
A very long URL with random characters
No 'About' page
quizQuestions.answer: A padlock icon, clear contact information, and professional content
quizQuestions.explanation: Trust signals include HTTPS (padlock), transparent contact information, professional design and grammar, and a clear 'About' page — all indicating a legitimate, credible site.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "HTTPS in a website address indicates:" is: The connection between your browser and the website is encrypted.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The connection between your browser and the website is encrypted is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The SIFT method for evaluating online information stands for:
options: Search, Investigate, Filter, Trust
Stop, Investigate the source, Find better coverage, Trace claims
Scan, Identify, Fact-check, Think
Source, Information, Facts, Truth
quizQuestions.answer: Stop, Investigate the source, Find better coverage, Trace claims
quizQuestions.explanation: SIFT is a four-step fact-checking framework: Stop (don't share immediately), Investigate the source (who made this?), Find better coverage (what do other sources say?), and Trace claims to originals.

quizQuestions.question: Lateral reading as a fact-checking strategy involves:
options: Reading an article from left to right very carefully
Opening new browser tabs to research what others say about the source or claim
Reading multiple articles on the same topic from the same website
Skimming an article quickly for key words
quizQuestions.answer: Opening new browser tabs to research what others say about the source or claim
quizQuestions.explanation: Lateral reading — leaving a site to search for information about it — is the strategy used by professional fact-checkers to quickly assess source credibility.

quizQuestions.question: Misinformation differs from disinformation in that misinformation:
options: Is always more dangerous
Is false information spread with intent to deceive
Is false or inaccurate information spread without intentional deception
Only exists on social media
quizQuestions.answer: Is false or inaccurate information spread without intentional deception
quizQuestions.explanation: Misinformation is inaccurate content spread accidentally; disinformation is deliberately false content created to deceive. Both cause harm, but intent differs.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which website is a reliable tool for checking the accuracy of viral claims and news stories."
quizQuestions.answer: Snopes.com
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Snopes.com.

quizQuestions.question: A 'cookie' on a website is:
options: A type of malware that damages your computer
A small text file stored by your browser that tracks your site visits and preferences
A required payment for accessing premium content
A security feature that blocks ads
quizQuestions.answer: A small text file stored by your browser that tracks your site visits and preferences
quizQuestions.explanation: Cookies store user data like login status, preferences, and browsing behavior — helpful for user experience but also used for tracking and targeted advertising.

quizQuestions.question: Which type of personal information should you NEVER share in a public online profile?
options: Your favorite TV show
Your full home address and phone number
Your general city of residence
Your educational interests
quizQuestions.answer: Your full home address and phone number
quizQuestions.explanation: Home address and phone number are sensitive personal identifiers that can be used to locate, contact, or exploit you — they should never appear in public online profiles.

quizQuestions.question: Data brokers are companies that:
options: Sell internet plans to consumers
Collect, analyze, and sell personal data about individuals to third parties
Manage data storage for businesses
Provide encryption services
quizQuestions.answer: Collect, analyze, and sell personal data about individuals to third parties
quizQuestions.explanation: Data brokers aggregate data from public records, social media, loyalty programs, and app usage to build detailed profiles on individuals — which they sell to advertisers, insurers, and others.

quizQuestions.question: A privacy policy on a website explains:
options: The website's political stance
How the website collects, uses, and shares your personal data
How much advertising the website displays
The technical specifications of the website
quizQuestions.answer: How the website collects, uses, and shares your personal data
quizQuestions.explanation: Privacy policies disclose what data is collected, why, how it is used, who it is shared with, and how long it is retained — important for understanding what you agree to.

quizQuestions.question: A strong password should include:
options: Only your name and birth year for easy remembering
At least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
The same password as other accounts for convenience
Only lowercase letters for fast typing
quizQuestions.answer: At least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
quizQuestions.explanation: Strong passwords are long and complex — combining character types makes them exponentially harder to crack through brute force or dictionary attacks.

quizQuestions.question: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) improves security by:
options: Requiring a longer password
Requiring a second proof of identity (like a phone code) in addition to a password
Blocking all international login attempts
Requiring you to change passwords every day
quizQuestions.answer: Requiring a second proof of identity (like a phone code) in addition to a password
quizQuestions.explanation: MFA adds a second verification step — even if your password is stolen, attackers cannot log in without also having your phone or biometric confirmation.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A strong password should include:" is: At least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. At least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Multi-factor authentication (MFA) improves security by:" is: Requiring a longer password.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: False
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Requiring a second proof of identity (like a phone code) in addition to a password.

quizQuestions.question: In Google Sheets, which formula calculates the sum of cells A1 through A10?
options: =TOTAL(A1:A10)
=ADD(A1,A10)
=SUM(A1:A10)
=CALC(A1-A10)
quizQuestions.answer: =SUM(A1:A10)
quizQuestions.explanation: The SUM function adds a range of cells together. =SUM(A1:A10) adds all values from cell A1 to A10 — one of the most fundamental spreadsheet formulas.

quizQuestions.question: The 'comment' feature in Google Docs is most useful for:
options: Deleting sections of text
Adding private notes to a document visible only to the author
Providing feedback and questions on a shared document without changing the text
Formatting text as bold or italic
quizQuestions.answer: Providing feedback and questions on a shared document without changing the text
quizQuestions.explanation: Comments allow collaborators to annotate text, ask questions, and suggest changes without altering the original content — essential for collaborative editing.

quizQuestions.question: An effective presentation slide should:
options: Contain as much text as possible to maximize information
Use large text, minimal bullet points, and one key idea per slide
Include as many animations as possible
Have a different font style on each slide
quizQuestions.answer: Use large text, minimal bullet points, and one key idea per slide
quizQuestions.explanation: Effective slides support the speaker rather than replace them — one idea per slide with large text and visuals keeps the audience engaged without overwhelming them.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "In Google Sheets, which formula calculates the sum of cells A1 through A10" is: =SUM(A1:A10).
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. =SUM(A1:A10) is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Netiquette refers to:
options: The technical rules for connecting to Wi-Fi networks
Etiquette and behavioral standards for online communication
The legal rules governing internet commerce
Software for managing email accounts
quizQuestions.answer: Etiquette and behavioral standards for online communication
quizQuestions.explanation: Netiquette is the informal code of conduct for respectful, appropriate behavior in digital communication — from email tone to social media behavior.

quizQuestions.question: Writing in ALL CAPS in online communication is generally interpreted as:
options: Being enthusiastic
Shouting or expressing extreme emotion
Writing a formal document
Emphasizing technical terms
quizQuestions.answer: Shouting or expressing extreme emotion
quizQuestions.explanation: All-caps text in digital communication is understood as shouting — a netiquette violation in most professional or collaborative contexts.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Netiquette refers to:" is: Etiquette and behavioral standards for online communication.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Etiquette and behavioral standards for online communication is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The Google search operator 'site:' allows you to:
options: Search only paid results
Limit results to a specific website or domain
Search for websites created on a specific date
Find exact word matches only
quizQuestions.answer: Limit results to a specific website or domain
quizQuestions.explanation: The site: operator (e.g., site:edu climate change) restricts search results to a specific domain — useful for searching within a particular organization or type of site.

quizQuestions.question: Google Scholar is a useful research tool because it:
options: Contains only Wikipedia articles
Searches academic journals, theses, and scholarly books
Shows only free articles from any website
Is the same as regular Google but with a different design
quizQuestions.answer: Searches academic journals, theses, and scholarly books
quizQuestions.explanation: Google Scholar indexes peer-reviewed papers, theses, court opinions, and academic books — making it far more useful than general Google for finding credible academic sources.

quizQuestions.question: Putting a phrase in quotation marks in a Google search:
options: Excludes those words from the results
Searches for the exact phrase in that specific order
Ranks results by date
Limits results to news articles only
quizQuestions.answer: Searches for the exact phrase in that specific order
quizQuestions.explanation: Quoted searches (e.g., "climate change policy") return results containing those exact words in that exact order — reducing irrelevant results.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The Google search operator 'site:' allows you to:" is: Limit results to a specific website or domain.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Limit results to a specific website or domain is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes a phishing attack?
options: Hacking into a network by exploiting software vulnerabilities
Deceiving people through fake messages or websites to steal personal information
Installing malware by connecting a USB drive
Overloading a server with traffic until it crashes
questions.answer: Deceiving people through fake messages or websites to steal personal information
questions.explanation: Phishing is a social engineering attack that uses deceptive communication — fake emails, websites, or texts — to trick victims into revealing credentials or financial information.

questions.question: The SIFT method advises you to 'Investigate the source' by:
options: Reading the article very carefully from beginning to end
Looking up who made the content and whether they are credible before reading further
Counting the number of sources cited in the article
Checking how many social media shares the article has
questions.answer: Looking up who made the content and whether they are credible before reading further
questions.explanation: Investigating the source means quickly checking who is behind the content before investing time reading it — a key habit that prevents being misled by unreliable sources.

questions.question: Adjusting your privacy settings on social media platforms helps to:
options: Increase the number of followers you have
Control who can see your personal information and content
Improve the quality of your internet connection
Prevent the platform from making money from advertising
questions.answer: Control who can see your personal information and content
questions.explanation: Privacy settings let you decide who can see your profile, posts, location, and other personal data — an essential step in managing your digital footprint.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is most important because:" is: Even if your password is stolen, a second factor prevents unauthorized access.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Even if your password is stolen, a second factor prevents unauthorized access is correct.

questions.question: In Google Sheets, which formula would calculate the average of cells B2 through B20?
options: =MEAN(B2:B20)
=AVERAGE(B2:B20)
=AVG(B2:B20)
questions.answer: =AVERAGE(B2:B20)
questions.explanation: The AVERAGE function calculates the arithmetic mean of a range. =AVERAGE(B2:B20) sums all values and divides by the count automatically.

questions.question: Lateral reading is a fact-checking strategy used by professional fact-checkers that involves:
options: Carefully reading every word of an article before forming an opinion
Immediately searching for information about the source from other websites
Reading only the left column of a Wikipedia article
Comparing the article to other articles on the same website
questions.answer: Immediately searching for information about the source from other websites
questions.explanation: Lateral reading means leaving a website to quickly check what other credible sources say about it — far more efficient than reading an unreliable site in detail.

questions.question: A Creative Commons CC BY-NC license allows others to:
options: Use the work commercially without credit
Use and modify the work non-commercially with attribution
Use the work for any purpose without any restrictions
Create derivative works and sell them
questions.answer: Use and modify the work non-commercially with attribution
questions.explanation: CC BY-NC means users must credit the creator (BY) and may only use the work for non-commercial (NC) purposes — a common license for educational content.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is an example of a passive digital footprint" is: A photo you post on Instagram.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Your location data collected by an app you use.

questions.question: A netiquette rule that applies to professional email communication is:
options: Always use lowercase letters for a casual tone
Include a descriptive subject line and professional greeting
Send emails at any time of day without considering the recipient's time zone
Use emojis to show friendliness in all professional contexts
questions.answer: Include a descriptive subject line and professional greeting
questions.explanation: A clear subject line helps recipients prioritize and find emails; a professional greeting sets the appropriate tone — both fundamental to effective professional email.

questions.question: The Google search operator that finds pages that link to a specific URL is:
options: site:
link:
inurl:
related:
questions.answer: link:
questions.explanation: The link: operator finds pages that contain links to a specific URL — useful for researching what sites reference a particular page.

questions.question: Social media algorithms primarily show you content that:
options: Is posted by accounts you have never interacted with
Maximizes engagement based on your past behavior
Is the most factually accurate available
Was posted most recently with no other factors
questions.answer: Maximizes engagement based on your past behavior
questions.explanation: Social media algorithms optimize for engagement (likes, shares, time spent) — showing content similar to what you have previously interacted with, regardless of its accuracy or diversity.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is the MOST secure approach to password management" is: Using a reputable password manager to generate and store unique passwords for each account.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Using a reputable password manager to generate and store unique passwords for each account is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the concept of a filter bubble?
options: A browser extension that blocks offensive content
An algorithm-driven information environment where users see only content matching their existing views
A cybersecurity tool that filters malicious emails
A privacy feature that hides your location from apps
questions.answer: An algorithm-driven information environment where users see only content matching their existing views
questions.explanation: Filter bubbles are created by personalization algorithms that limit information exposure — users see increasingly narrow, confirmatory content rather than a diverse range of perspectives.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic of misinformation as opposed to disinformation?
options: Misinformation is always more widely shared
Misinformation is spread without intent to deceive, while disinformation is deliberately false
Misinformation only occurs on social media platforms
Misinformation is always created by governments
questions.answer: Misinformation is spread without intent to deceive, while disinformation is deliberately false
questions.explanation: The distinction is intent: misinformation is false content shared accidentally (by people who believe it or didn't check); disinformation is deliberately created to manipulate.

questions.question: Being a responsible digital citizen when you encounter cyberbullying means:
options: Participating if most of your friends are involved
Ignoring it because it is not your problem
Reporting it to the platform and, if appropriate, to a trusted adult
Retaliating against the bully online
questions.answer: Reporting it to the platform and, if appropriate, to a trusted adult
questions.explanation: Digital citizenship involves being an upstander — using reporting tools to flag harassment and seeking adult help for serious cases, rather than participating or ignoring it.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A ransomware attack can BEST be prevented by:" is: Using a very long password.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Regularly backing up data to an offline location and keeping software updated.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about AI language models is TRUE?
options: They always produce factually accurate information
They cannot generate creative writing
They generate text based on statistical patterns and can produce plausible but incorrect content
They have real-time access to all internet information
questions.answer: They generate text based on statistical patterns and can produce plausible but incorrect content
questions.explanation: LLMs generate text by predicting likely word sequences based on training data — they do not 'know' facts and can generate convincingly written but incorrect information (hallucinations).

questions.question: Which type of tracking would continue even if you deleted all your browser cookies?
options: Session cookies
Browser fingerprinting, which identifies your device by its unique configuration
First-party cookies
Third-party cookies
questions.answer: Browser fingerprinting, which identifies your device by its unique configuration
questions.explanation: Browser fingerprinting tracks users through unique combinations of browser settings, installed fonts, screen resolution, and other attributes — working even without cookies.

questions.question: When evaluating the 'Currency' of a source using the CRAAP test, you are assessing:
options: How much the article cost to produce
Whether the information is recent enough for your research topic
Whether the source accepts payment for publishing
How popular the source is currently
questions.answer: Whether the information is recent enough for your research topic
questions.explanation: Currency evaluates the date of information — critical for fast-changing fields like medicine or technology where old information may be incorrect.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The Internet of Things (IoT) raises which privacy concern" is: Connected devices continuously collect and transmit data about your behavior and environment.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Connected devices continuously collect and transmit data about your behavior and environment is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is an ethical use of an AI writing tool for a school assignment?
options: Submitting the AI's output verbatim as your essay
Using AI to generate practice questions to test yourself, then writing your own response
Having AI write your entire paper and changing a few words
Using AI to translate your assignment from another language you wrote it in to avoid a language class
questions.answer: Using AI to generate practice questions to test yourself, then writing your own response
questions.explanation: Using AI as a study tool — generating questions, explanations, or feedback — supports your own learning without misrepresenting your work as something you didn't produce.

questions.question: The 'Purpose' criterion in the CRAAP test evaluates:
options: Whether the article has a clear purpose statement
Why the information was created — to inform, persuade, sell, entertain, or manipulate
Whether the article is long enough to be useful
How many purposes the article serves
questions.answer: Why the information was created — to inform, persuade, sell, entertain, or manipulate
questions.explanation: Understanding the purpose behind content reveals potential bias — advertising content, advocacy websites, and satire all have different motivations that affect how their information should be interpreted.

questions.question: Fair use is most likely to apply when you:
options: Post an entire movie on YouTube for free
Quote two sentences from a published book to support an argument in your own essay
Sell printed copies of a copyrighted photograph
Create a website using a copyrighted logo without permission
questions.answer: Quote two sentences from a published book to support an argument in your own essay
questions.explanation: Fair use typically applies to small amounts of copyrighted material used for commentary, criticism, or education — transformative academic use of brief quotations is the clearest example.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is the BEST strategy for maintaining digital well-being" is: Avoiding all technology except for school assignments.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Setting intentional boundaries around technology use while maintaining engagement for meaningful purposes.

questions.question: When using Google Scholar to find sources, you should prioritize articles that are:
options: The most recently cited articles regardless of their content
Peer-reviewed and published in reputable academic journals
Only available as free full-text downloads
Written by the most famous authors in the field
questions.answer: Peer-reviewed and published in reputable academic journals
questions.explanation: Peer review is the key quality indicator for academic sources — it means the work was evaluated by independent subject-matter experts before publication.

questions.question: Which practice BEST reduces the spread of misinformation online?
options: Only sharing content from accounts you follow
Pausing before sharing, verifying the claim from independent sources, and sharing responsibly
Sharing widely to let others decide what is true
Trusting content that aligns with your existing knowledge
questions.answer: Pausing before sharing, verifying the claim from independent sources, and sharing responsibly
questions.explanation: Most misinformation spreads because people share without checking. Pausing and verifying — even briefly — dramatically reduces your role in spreading false information.

questions.question: A positive digital footprint can benefit you by:
options: Preventing any organization from finding information about you
Showcasing your skills, achievements, and responsible online behavior to future employers or colleges
Making your social media accounts visible to more followers
Reducing the amount of data companies can collect about you
questions.answer: Showcasing your skills, achievements, and responsible online behavior to future employers or colleges
questions.explanation: A curated positive digital footprint — portfolios, thoughtful posts, professional profiles — can strengthen college applications and job searches by demonstrating character and competence.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Automation's impact on the job market is BEST described as:" is: Transforming many jobs by automating routine tasks while creating new roles requiring human skills.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Transforming many jobs by automating routine tasks while creating new roles requiring human skills is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most effective defense against social engineering attacks?
options: Installing the latest antivirus software
Educating users to recognize and verify suspicious requests before complying
Using very complex passwords
Never using email for communication
questions.answer: Educating users to recognize and verify suspicious requests before complying
questions.explanation: Social engineering exploits human psychology rather than technical vulnerabilities — education and a habit of verification (calling back on known numbers, checking with supervisors) are the primary defenses.

questions.question: Digital citizenship responsibilities include:
options: Sharing as much personal information as possible to build your online presence
Respecting intellectual property, protecting your privacy, and treating others with respect online
Maximizing your time online to stay current
Sharing any information that seems important, regardless of its accuracy
questions.answer: Respecting intellectual property, protecting your privacy, and treating others with respect online
questions.explanation: The nine elements of digital citizenship include digital rights and responsibilities, ethics, security, privacy, and literacy — all centered on informed, ethical, and respectful online participation.

questions.question: Deepfakes pose a particular risk to society because they:
options: Make movies look more realistic
Use AI to create convincing fake images or videos of real people, potentially spreading disinformation
Require expensive equipment that limits their production
Can only be created by professional filmmakers
questions.answer: Use AI to create convincing fake images or videos of real people, potentially spreading disinformation
questions.explanation: Deepfakes can falsely portray real people saying or doing things they never did — with potential to damage reputations, spread political disinformation, and undermine trust in video evidence.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which presentation design principle improves audience comprehension" is: Using as many different fonts as possible for variety.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Using visuals and minimal text to complement — not replace — the speaker's words.

questions.question: The 'right to be forgotten' is most associated with which value?
options: Freedom of speech online
The right to have outdated or harmful personal data removed from online databases
The right to anonymous online communication
The right to copy any content you find online
questions.answer: The right to have outdated or harmful personal data removed from online databases
questions.explanation: The right to be forgotten (erasure) allows individuals to request removal of personal data that is no longer relevant or is causing harm — recognized in GDPR and growing globally.

questions.question: Which skill is considered MOST future-proof as technology continues to advance?
options: Proficiency in one specific programming language
The ability to quickly learn new tools and adapt to technological change
Memorizing technical specifications of current devices
Only working in jobs that cannot be computerized
questions.answer: The ability to quickly learn new tools and adapt to technological change
questions.explanation: In a rapidly changing technological landscape, adaptability and continuous learning are the most durable skills — specific tool knowledge may become obsolete while the ability to learn remains valuable.

questions.question: Which of the following BEST summarizes the responsibility of digital literacy for democracy?
options: Digital literacy is only important for personal safety
Critically evaluating online information is essential for making informed civic and political decisions
Government agencies should decide what information citizens can access online
Digital literacy skills are only necessary for people who use social media
questions.answer: Critically evaluating online information is essential for making informed civic and political decisions
questions.explanation: Democracy depends on informed citizens — as more civic discourse and political information moves online, the ability to evaluate sources and resist manipulation becomes essential for democratic participation.
